message:
feat(frankenpandas): expand prelude to cover README Quick Start (br-pg29m / fd90.121)

fd90.121: README Quick Start at lines 209-234 imports
'use frankenpandas::prelude::*;' then calls write_csv_string,
write_json_string, write_sql, read_sql_table, plus the SqlIfExists
type. None of these were in the prelude — the documented entry point
for users — so a copy-paste of the example would hit five E0425 /
E0412 errors.

Fix: add the missing items to prelude. Specifically:
  - readers: read_sql, read_sql_table
  - writers: write_csv_string, write_json_string, write_jsonl_string,
             write_sql
  - SQL contracts: SqlConnection, SqlIfExists

Verified:
- cargo build -p frankenpandas — compiles
- cargo test -p frankenpandas --lib — all 5 prelude tests pass
  (prelude_smoke_test, prelude_concat_works, prelude_query_works,
   prelude_io_roundtrip, prelude_to_datetime_works)

The items were already pub-used at the top-level frankenpandas crate
(crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs:51-147), so this is a pure prelude
expansion — no behavior change, just easier discovery for users.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -617,6 +617,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-pavu","title":"[HIGH] fuzz: cross-format round-trip parity fuzz target (catch IO+Equality drift)","description":"Existing fuzz harness has individual format round-trip targets (excel/parquet/feather/ipc_stream/json/csv) but each only checks 'parse → write → reparse → equals'. None of them check CROSS-FORMAT parity: parquet write of a frame should produce data that, when written through arrow IPC, contains the same logical values. Recent IO drift bugs (br-989b excel index leak, br-mxho fuzz excel leak, plus calamine 0.34.0 dependency bump) suggest format-specific assumptions can silently diverge.

Proposed target: fuzz_format_cross_round_trip.rs:
1. Synth mode: project bytes into a small DataFrame (3-8 rows, 2-3 typed columns).
2. Pick a primary format (parquet/feather/ipc_stream) from input bytes.
3. Round-trip through primary format → frame_a.
4. Round-trip through a SECONDARY format → frame_b.
5. Assert frame_a.semantic_eq(frame_b) (cross-format invariant: the lossy bits of any individual format must converge to the same logical content).
6. Assert no panic on any byte input.

New fp-conformance pub fn fuzz_format_cross_round_trip_bytes(input: &[u8]). Catches the entire class of drift where format X loses metadata that format Y preserves.","notes":"cc-pandas triage 2026-04-22: implemented in fuzz_targets/fuzz_format_cross_round_trip.rs + fp_conformance::fuzz_format_cross_round_trip_bytes (lib.rs:5138). Synthesizes a frame, round-trips through a primary FuzzArrowFormat, then through a distinct secondary format, asserts frame-level parity via DataFrame::equals. Landed in commit 49f0cdf.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:24:44.631990108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:33:49.204576948Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:33:49.201998451Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-pbh9","title":"fp-columnar Column.fillna_with_column parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:30:42.323915804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:35:39.046032956Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:35:39.044217778Z","close_reason":"Implemented Column::fillna_with_column in commit 178e515, 1 test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-pc4","title":"Add DataFrame GroupBy.get_group conformance test coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T08:31:51.516284535Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T08:33:35.852135053Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T08:33:35.849856219Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-pg29m","title":"fd90.121: README Quick Start uses prelude::* but write_*_string/write_sql/read_sql_table not in prelude","description":"fd90.74-120 README audit. Quick Start at lines 209-234 imports 'use frankenpandas::prelude::*;' then calls:
  write_csv_string(&by_ticker)?
  write_json_string(&by_ticker, JsonOrient::Records)?
  write_feather_bytes(&by_ticker)?
  write_sql(&by_ticker, &conn, "results", SqlIfExists::Fail)?
  read_sql_table(&conn, "results")?

But crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs:171-211 prelude only re-exports a curated subset:
  read_csv_str, read_feather_bytes, read_json_str, read_jsonl_str,
  read_parquet_bytes, write_feather_bytes, write_parquet_bytes
  (plus to_datetime, to_numeric, to_timedelta and the type set)

Missing from prelude: write_csv_string, write_json_string, write_jsonl_string,
write_sql, read_sql_table, SqlIfExists, SqlConnection. These ARE pub-used at
the top-level frankenpandas crate (lib.rs:51-147), so 'use frankenpandas::*'
would work, but the documented entry point for users is prelude::*.

A user copy-pasting Quick Start would hit:
  error[E0425]: cannot find function 'write_csv_string' in this scope
  error[E0425]: cannot find function 'write_json_string' in this scope
  error[E0425]: cannot find function 'write_sql' in this scope
  error[E0425]: cannot find function 'read_sql_table' in this scope
  error[E0412]: cannot find type 'SqlIfExists' in this scope

Fix: expand the prelude to cover all writers used in README's documented examples. Specifically add:
  write_csv_string, write_json_string, write_jsonl_string,
  read_sql, read_sql_table, write_sql, SqlIfExists, SqlConnection

Verify with cargo build -p frankenpandas. Code change to fp-frankenpandas; pure prelude expansion, no behavior change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:20:40.284325316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:22:27.711924532Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:22:27.709291505Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-phum","title":"fd90.23: SqlConnection::truncate_table for fast table reset","description":"Add SqlConnection::truncate_table(table, schema) -> Result<(), IoError> for fast clear-without-drop semantics. Default impl uses DELETE FROM (universal but slower). PostgreSQL/MySQL backends override with TRUNCATE TABLE for cheap full-table reset. Schema arg routes through quote_identifier and supports_schemas gating. Free-fn truncate_sql_table wrapper. Tests cover SQLite default DELETE behavior, multi-schema stub routing, schema-Some on SQLite (silently ignored).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T20:34:04.250016433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T20:36:41.666166269Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T20:36:41.663645679Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fd90","review","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-pizo3","title":"fd90.119: README df.xs("string") wrong — xs takes &IndexLabel not &str","description":"fd90.74-118 README audit. Line 521 in DataFrame Output Formats shows:

  let row = df.xs("2024-01-15")?;

But crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:26531 declares:
  pub fn xs(&self, key: &IndexLabel) -> Result<Self, FrameError>

xs takes &IndexLabel, not &str. A user copy-pasting hits:
  error[E0308]: expected reference '&IndexLabel', found '&str'

Fix: use the From<&str> blanket on IndexLabel (verified at crates/fp-index/src/lib.rs:97). Two sensible forms:
  df.xs(&IndexLabel::Utf8("2024-01-15".into()))?
  df.xs(&"2024-01-15".into())?

Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:12:35.541346999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:13:41.619292068Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:13:41.616572156Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-pl4","title":"Add DataFrame assign packet conformance coverage","description":"Testing-conformance fallback audit found DataFrame::assign and assign_column implemented in fp-frame but no dataframe_assign fixture operation or packet coverage. Add pandas-oracle-backed FP-P2D fixtures for appending a direct-value column, overwriting an existing column, preserving ordered multi-assignment output, and rejecting length mismatch so pandas df.assign parity is frozen.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:49:41.140001375Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T02:55:55.115828563Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T02:55:55.114131621Z","close_reason":"Completed DataFrame assign conformance packet coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","dataframe","mutation"]}

@@ -192,21 +192,32 @@ pub mod prelude {
         Scalar,
         Series,
         SeriesResetIndexResult,
+        // SQL contracts (covers the README Quick Start round-trip)
+        SqlConnection,
+        SqlIfExists,
         // Module-level functions
         concat_dataframes,
         concat_series,
         merge_ordered,
-        // IO
+        // IO — readers
         read_csv_str,
         read_feather_bytes,
         read_json_str,
         read_jsonl_str,
         read_parquet_bytes,
+        read_sql,
+        read_sql_table,
+        // IO — datetime helpers
         to_datetime,
         to_numeric,
         to_timedelta,
+        // IO — writers (string + bytes + sql, matching README documented surface)
+        write_csv_string,
         write_feather_bytes,
+        write_json_string,
+        write_jsonl_string,
         write_parquet_bytes,
+        write_sql,
     };
 }
